verificationLabel: Round 9 team lists checked Tuesday 29 Apr 2026
summary: Round 9 opens with Bulldogs the cleanest early lean, a healthier Broncos side back in the frame, and the charity bank still waiting for a value play after the no-bet Round 8.

scoreboard.note: AJ Brimson and Chris Randall are back for Gold Coast while Canberra remain light in the back row after the loss of Zac Hosking and Noah Martin.
Canterbury lose Viliame Kikau but still look more settled, while North Queensland must replace Murray Taulagi on the wing.
Parramatta stay unchanged while the Warriors get Adam Pompey back and list Luke Metcalf on the interchange.
Melbourne still carry volatility, but Harry Grant and the same core group give them the steadier read against a Dolphins pack that is still reshuffling.
Brisbane suddenly look a lot more dangerous with Walsh, Mam, Reynolds, Carrigan and Cory Paix all named.
Kalyn Ponga, Bradman Best, Greg Marzhew and Tyson Frizell all return, making Newcastle far more trustworthy than last week's version.
Cronulla stay largely settled while the Tigers continue to juggle their depth around the edges and bench.
Penrith roll in unchanged while Manly still have no Turbo and lose Corey Waddell from the side that beat Parramatta.

liveAlerts.text: Round 9 team lists are now out, so the board can move from the Round 8 wrap into real tipping mode.
The charity bank stays at $6.50 after the disciplined no-bet Round 8.
Bulldogs over Cowboys looks like the cleanest straight tip, but the price may decide whether it is usable for charity.
Broncos and Knights both get major names back, which makes their matches harder than the raw formline suggests.
Turbo remains out and keeps Manly from being treated like a normal four-win side.
No Round 9 charity bet is locked yet because the value layer still needs market and late-mail confirmation.
